serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 300 g (one bowl), typical of a single-portion noodle dish served in Singapore hawker centres and Chinese restaurants.

nutrient_source.title: Dry Noodle Tossed in Scallion Oil with Shredded Pork — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this dish (cr_id G00010059). The HPB record reports carbohydrate as 0.0 g/100g, which is nutritionally inconsistent with a wheat-noodle dish. This anomaly is documented in notes. All other values are preserved as reported.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for scallion oil noodles with shredded pork. GI of 52 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for wheat noodles (Chinese-style, boiled), which reports GI approximately 47–58 (mean ~52). The dish is predominantly wheat noodles by weight, making this the most defensible analogous anchor. Glycemic load per serving is reported as 0.0 because the HPB source records carbohydrate_g as 0.0 g/100g; this is a known data anomaly (see notes). A corrected GL estimate based on typical wheat noodle carbohydrate content (~25 g per 300 g serving cooked) would be approximately GL 13, but this cannot be substituted without overriding the approved source value.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1050 mg/100g — far exceeds the 600 mg/100g upper threshold
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this dish; sodium at 1050 mg/100g contributes over 70% of the 1500 mg daily sodium target in a single 300 g serving, and the low potassium content (47 mg/100g) provides no meaningful offset.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Wheat noodles are high-GI refined carbohydrates with negligible fibre; HPB-recorded carbohydrate of 0 g is a known data anomaly
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this as a high-carbohydrate dish (typical wheat noodle dishes contain 25–35 g carbohydrate per 300 g serving); request wholemeal or lower-GI noodle alternatives, limit portion size, and pair with non-starchy vegetables to blunt postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium (1050 mg/100g), moderate fat (10.5 g/100g), and cholesterol (89 mg/100g) from pork
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit this dish due to its high sodium content driving hypertensive risk, combined with moderate fat and cholesterol from shredded pork; if consumed, restrict to a small portion and avoid adding additional soy sauce or condiments.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High sodium, pork-derived arachidonic acid, and refined wheat noodles with high glycaemic load
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should limit this dish; pork contributes arachidonic acid (omega-6) that amplifies inflammatory cytokine pathways, refined wheat noodles increase glycaemic load and systemic inflammation, and sodium at 1050 mg/100g exceeds the RA dietary threshold.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Dry Noodle Tossed in Scallion Oil with Shredded Pork (肉丝干拌葱油面, Ròu Sī Gān Bàn Cōng Yóu Miàn) is a Chinese-style dry noodle dish popular in Singapore hawker centres and Chinese restaurants. Springy wheat noodles are boiled, drained, and tossed — rather than served in broth — with fragrant scallion-infused oil and a house-made seasoning sauce typically containing soy sauce, oyster sauce, and sesame oil. The dish is topped with tender shredded pork and crispy fried shallots, which add textural contrast and savoury depth. The flavour profile is rich, umami-forward, and aromatic, with the scallion oil providing a distinctive fragrance. Per 100 g, the HPB Singapore database records 171 kcal, 19.1 g protein, 10.5 g fat, and 1050 mg sodium. The carbohydrate value is recorded as 0.0 g in the HPB source, which is a known data anomaly inconsistent with wheat noodles as the primary ingredient; typical cooked wheat noodles contribute approximately 25–35 g carbohydrate per 300 g serving. Clinically, the dish is high concern for hypertension (sodium 1050 mg/100g), Type 2 Diabetes (high-GI refined carbohydrates), and cardiovascular disease (combined sodium, fat, and cholesterol load). It is not halal due to pork content. Allergens include gluten and soy.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010059, confidence 0.95), which is the primary approved source for Singapore dishes. The carbohydrate anomaly (0.0 g) is documented but does not reduce source confidence as the HPB record is the canonical approved source for this dish.
notes: CARBOHYDRATE ANOMALY: carbohydrate_g is recorded as 0.0 g/100g in the HPB source. This is nutritionally inconsistent with a wheat-noodle dish, which typically contributes 25–35 g carbohydrate per 300 g cooked serving. The 0.0 value is preserved as reported from the approved source (HPB Singapore Foodo) and flagged for upstream correction. Sugar_g is also 0.0 g as reported by HPB; this is consistent with the carbohydrate anomaly and similarly flagged. Dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 g as reported by HPB; this is also inconsistent with wheat noodles and is flagged. ZERO-MACRO JUSTIFICATION: carbohydrate_g = 0.0 is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo source but is a suspected data entry anomaly — not naturally zero. Sugar_g = 0.0 is confirmed zero per HPB source, consistent with the carbohydrate anomaly. Dietary_fibre_g = 0.0 is confirmed zero per HPB source, also suspected anomaly. NEGATIVE SENTINEL VALUES: saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown, vitamin profile, and moisture data for this specific dish preparation are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for G00010059. GI NOTE: GI of 52 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database wheat noodle (Chinese-style, boiled) data. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 due to the HPB carbohydrate anomaly; a corrected GL based on typical wheat noodle composition would be approximately 13 for a 300 g serving. HALAL STATUS: This dish contains pork and is not halal.
